白 (white — looks like 日 sun with a slanting ray on top, the pale dawn light) + 色 (color). White color.
黄 (yellow — originally a pictograph of a person wearing a yellow jade ornament around the waist) + 色 (color). The color of ripe grain and jade.
绿 (green — 纟 silk + 录 record, silk dyed the color of fresh leaves) + 色 (color). Green color.
蓝 (blue — 艹 plant + 监 oversee, the indigo plant tended for blue dye) + 色 (color). Blue color.

口 (mouth) + 巴 (a tail/end shape). A sentence-final particle that softens commands or makes suggestions: 走吧 'let's go', 好吧 'okay then'.
Your mouth asking like a horse neighing for attention.
口 (mouth) + 尼 (a kneeling figure / phonetic). A sentence-final question particle. 你呢? = 'And you?'
A mouth (口) calling out to someone nearby or on a line.
Top is 匕 (a bowing figure) over 目 (eye) over 八 (legs). A figure peering down with the eye to verify the TRUE form. Real, truly.
A fierce person taking a step to emphasize an point.
曰 (say/declare) + 取 (take — 耳 ear + 又 hand). When you declare 'I'll TAKE this one!' — that's the MOST/best.
Shifting the sun to indicate even more light is coming.
A big (大) person with an extra drop or dot, indicating an excessive or extreme amount.
A mouth (口) with legs. Used for a single animal.
尚 (esteem/honor) + 巾 (cloth/banner). The honored banner that's ALWAYS hanging — always/often. Common in 常常 'often' and 平常 'usually'.
京 (capital — a tall building) + 尤 (especially). Going right up to the capital, especially this one — exactly/just/then.
A funnel or vessel used to add more. 'I'll have some ALSO.'
辶 (walk) + 不 (not). 'Not yet done walking' — still going, still more. (Same character but pronounced huán means 'to return'.)
A pictograph of stacked horizontal layers — doing something one MORE time. 再见 = see you again.
Top portion 牛-like + 儿 (legs). A person striding out ahead of the herd — FIRST.
廿 (twenty, originally two tens together) + 八 (two hands raised). Many hands joining together — totaling, sharing in common.
Using a knife (刂) to separate the mouth (口) and strength (力) from others. DON'T do that!
A silk thread (纟) connecting two people as an item is handed over.
Speaking (讠) words that allow someone else to rise up (上) and pass. Yielding.
A hand (扌) reaching out to grasp a handle (巴).
Clothes (衤) that have been caught on a hook (皮). A passive occurrence.
One person (人) following directly behind another person (人) from the start.
Two people standing back to back to compare their heights.
Reaching a place (至) and using a knife (刂) to mark your arrival.
A mouth facing a specific direction through a window.
亠 + 凶 (ill omen) + 禸 (a creature). A creature departing because of an ill omen — to leave, to be away from.
A pictograph of a wooden bucket with a handle through the middle — a useful tool you grab and 'USE'.
Originally a hand holding an implement, then flipped: a person 人 to the right of a tool 㠯. 'With' or 'by means of'.
Originally a pictograph of a hand 爪 leading an elephant by the trunk — laboring for a purpose. To do, to act for. (Also pronounced wéi 'to be/become'.)
囗 (enclosure) + 大 (big person). A big person stuck inside the boundaries of a situation — the CAUSE/reason hemming them in.
The place behind the door (户) where the axe (斤) is kept.
Grain (禾) in everyone's mouth (口). When everyone is fed, there is peace and togetherness.
Your feet (足) following the heels of another.
Still (还) is (是) the same situation.
Maybe (或) the person (者) will choose another way.
Can (可) it really be (是) true?
And (而) also (且) there is more.
Not (不) only (但) this one, but another too.
Tongue (舌) + sweetness (甘, an old form of 'sweet'). Your tongue (舌) tastes something sweet (甘). The 甘 component itself is a mouth with a dot inside — a tasty morsel held in the mouth.
Wine jar (酉) + walking slowly (夋). Wine left in the jar (酉, the same component seen in 酒) eventually turns sour like vinegar — time walks (夋) past and the flavor sours.
Grass/herb (艹) on top + old (古) below. Old, dried-out herbs taste BITTER. Also used figuratively for bitter hardship.
Bitter/pungent (辛, a tattooing needle radical = sharp pain) + bundle (束). A bundle of sharp pungent peppers — your tongue stings as if pricked by needles.
Water (氵) + first/before (先). Use water FIRST, before anything else — to wash your hands or face.
刷 (brush — a hand with a cloth and knife 刂 scraping) + 牙 (teeth — looks like interlocking molars). To 'brush the teeth'.
洗 (wash) + 澡 (water 氵 + many mouths 品 + tree 木 — birds in a tree splashing in water). To wash the whole body.
To move (运) and keep moving (动) with purpose.
Both characters use 钅(metal) + fire-related elements: forging/refining metal in fire. To exercise is to FORGE the body like metal — tempered and refined by heat and effort.
Grass/herb (艹) on top + 约 (bind/agreement) below. Medicine is herbs (艹) bound together into a pill — and a doctor's promise (约) to heal you.
上 (up/start) + 班 (shift, two jade pieces with a knife between — a divided shift). To go UP to your shift = clock in for work.
下 (down/off) + 班 (shift). To come DOWN from your shift = clock out.
出 (go out — stacked mountain peaks moving outward) + 差 (errand/dispatch — 羊 sheep + 工 work, like being sent off shepherd-style). Sent out on an errand = a business trip.
工 (work, a carpenter's tool) + 资 (resources/capital, with 贝 shell-money inside). The money-resources you get FROM your work.
公 (public — eight 八 divided fairly with a mouth) + 司 (manage/take charge). A 'publicly managed' organization — a company.
经 (manage/pass through, silk 纟thread that weaves the operation) + 理 (reason/order, 王 jade made orderly by polishing). One who manages with logic and order — the manager.
同 (same — one mouth 口 under one roof) + 事 (affair/work, a hand holding a brush over records). Same-work-person = colleague.
会 (gather — people under a roof) + 议 (discuss — words 讠 about righteousness 义). People gather to discuss = a meeting.
An electric (电) brain (脑) that thinks much faster than ours.
电子 (electron — electric 电 + small/child 子) + 邮件 (mail item — post 邮 + item 件). Literally 'electron-mail-item' = email.
手 (hand — five-fingered shape) + 机 (machine — wood 木 + a few 几). A machine you hold in your hand = mobile phone.
Look at the character itself — a literal pictograph of a fishing NET with crisscrossing threads inside a frame. Now extended to mean the internet.
网 (net/internet) + 站 (station — a person 立 standing 占). A 'station on the net' = a website.
密 (secret/dense — something hidden 宀 in a mountain 山 with heart 必) + 码 (code/number — stone 石 + horse 马, like numbered stones). A secret code of numbers = password.
消 (water 氵+ small 肖, to dissolve/disappear) + 息 (breath — self 自 + heart 心, the breath of the heart). Breath that disappears into the air = a message passing along.
告 (announce — ox 牛 + mouth 口, the bull bellows the news) + 诉 (state — words 讠+ near 斥). To announce in words = tell someone.
注 (pour — water 氵+ master 主) + 意 (intent — sound 音 over heart 心). Pour your intent into something = pay attention to it.
照 (illuminate — sun 日 + bright/clear 召 with fire 灬 below) + 顾 (look after — head 页 turning to look back). To shine on someone and keep looking after them = take care of.
习 (practice — a bird flapping its wings, repeated motion) + 惯 (used to — heart 忄+ to thread/string 贯). Repeated motions strung together in your heart = habit.
要 (want/important — 西 cover + 女 woman) + 求 (seek — a tasseled robe being requested). To seek what is wanted = a requirement or request.
完 (finish — roof 宀 over origin 元, returning home is to finish) + 成 (achieve — a halberd 戈 marking the moment success is achieved). Finished and achieved = completed.
成 (achieve — halberd 戈 marking completion) + 绩 (achievement — 纟 silk + 责 responsibility, the woven cloth result of responsible work). The final achievement = your result/grade.
之 (literary genitive 'of') + 前 (front). 'Of the front-time' = before.
Walking (辶) away into the past (过+去).
Returning (回) and coming (来) to the starting point.
Exiting (出) and moving away (去).

Both characters share 口 (mouth): 咳 = 口 + 亥 (phonetic); 嗽 = 口 + 欶 (suck/breathe in). Sharp sucking-and-hacking mouth sounds = coughing.
月 (flesh) + 庄 (manor/village). The grimy flesh of a peasant from the manor — dirty. (Note: 心脏 'heart' uses this same 脏 to mean 'organ'.)
高 (tall, abbreviated) on top + 几 (legs) below. A tall lamp-tower on legs — shining bright.
日 (sun) + 音 (sound). When the sun is only a 'sound' (no longer seen) — it has set and everything is DARK.
阝(hill on the left) + 月 (moon). The shady side of the hill where only the moon shines — cloudy/dark. This is the yin of yin-yang.
日 (sun) + 青 (blue-green/clear). A sun in a clear blue sky = sunny weather.
An insect or object being swirled around inside a large gust of wind.
A pictograph: a flat cloud at the top, with four raindrops falling underneath through the sky. The horizontal stroke is the sky/cloud, the vertical line is rain falling, and the dots are raindrops.
雨 (rain on top) + 彐 (a hand below). Snow is rain you can sweep up by hand — frozen rain.
Two horizontal strokes (the sky) with a curving shape below — a curling puff of cloud drifting in the air. Originally pictured as a swirling vapor rising.
月 (moon — crescent shape) + 亮 (bright — a tall tower 高 + child catching light below). Literally 'moon-bright' = the bright moon in the sky.
星 (star) = 日 (sun) + 生 (grow/born). A 'small sun' born in the night sky. The doubling adds an affectionate, twinkling feel.
地 (earth/ground — soil 土 + also 也) + 球 (ball/sphere — jade 王 + seek 求). The earth-ball = the planet Earth.
世 (generation/era — three tens 十 stacked, ~30 years per generation) + 界 (boundary — field 田 + intervene 介). All generations across all boundaries = the world.
国 (nation — jade 玉 enclosed by a border 囗, a country is treasure within walls) + 家 (home — pig 豕 under a roof 宀). The 'nation-home' is your country.
城 (wall — soil 土 + achievement 成, walls of earth built up) + 市 (market). A walled marketplace = city.
地 (ground — 土 soil + 也) + 方 (square/direction). A square patch of ground = a place.
街 (street — 行 walk/go split around 圭 jade pavers) + 道 (way/road — 辶 walk + 首 head). A walking-way through town.
路 (road — 足 foot + 各 each) + 口 (mouth/opening). The mouth/opening of a road = intersection.
公 (public — 八 'divided fairly' + 厶) + 园 (garden — enclosure 囗 + 元 origin). A garden open to the public = park.
A shop (店) where you can sit down for a meal (饭).
A place where merchants (商) set up their shops (店) to trade.
超 (exceed/super — 走 walk past + 召 call) + 市 (market). A market that exceeds an ordinary market = supermarket.
sentence_en: Go to supermarket to buy food.

mnemonic: 银 (silver — 钅 metal + 艮) + 行 (firm/business; also 'walk'). The silver business = a bank. Note 行 is read háng here, not xíng.
A large courtyard or building (院) dedicated to medicine (医).
A place of study (学) surrounded by trees (校/木) where students meet.
厕 (toilet — 厂 shelter + 则 rule) + 所 (place — 户 door + 斤 axe). A regulated little walled spot = the toilet.
sentence_en: I want to go to the toilet.

mnemonic: 办 (handle/do — 力 strength flanked by two strokes for tools) + 公 (public affairs) + 室 (room — 宀 roof + 至 arrive). The room for handling public affairs = office.
会议 (meeting — gather + discuss) + 室 (room). The room where meetings happen.
厨 (kitchen — 厂 shelter + 豆 bean/food + 寸 hand) + 房 (room — 户 door + 方 square). The room where hands prepare food = kitchen.
卧 (lie down — 臣 a bowing minister + 卜) + 室 (room). The room where you lie down = bedroom.
洗 (wash — 氵 water + 先 first) + 手 (hand) + 间 (room — 门 door + 日 sun). The 'wash-hand room' = polite Chinese term for bathroom.
客 (guest — 宀 roof + 各 each one welcomed) + 厅 (hall — 厂 shelter + 丁 nail). The hall for receiving guests = living room.
sentence_en: Watching TV in the living room.

mnemonic: 饭 (meal — 饣 food radical + 反) + 厅 (hall). The hall for meals = dining room.
The sun rising behind the trees in the forest. East.
Luxuriant vegetation hanging down in the warm southern sun.
A bird returning to its square nest as the sun sets in the west.
Two people sitting back to back to stay warm in the cold northern wind.
最 (most — 曰 say + 取 take, the one you declare 'take this!') + 后 (back/after). The 'most-after' position = finally, last.
Achieving (成) great merit and result (功).
Losing (失) the shells/money and suffering defeat (败).
Examining (检) and looking into (查) the details.
Setting a standard (准) and completing the equipment (备) for a task.
Opening (开) the path to start (始) the journey.
Tying a knot (结) to bind (束) the work together at the end.
休 (rest — 亻 person + 木 tree, leaning against a tree) + 息 (breath — 自 nose + 心 heart, the breath of the heart). Lean back and breathe = rest.
铅 (lead — 钅 metal + 㕣 phonetic) + 笔 (writing brush — ⺮ bamboo + 毛 fur). A bamboo-handled tool with a lead tip = pencil.
橡 (rubber tree — 木 tree + 象 elephant, the elephant-leg-like rubber tree) + 皮 (skin/leather). A sheet of rubber-tree skin = eraser.
尺 (a pictograph: 尸 hand/figure + 乀, ancient measure of a forearm length) + 子 (suffix). A measuring stick = ruler.
本 (root/volume — 木 tree with a mark at the root, also means 'a volume' since books were once on tree bark) + 子 (suffix). A small bound volume = notebook.
剪 (cut — 前 in front + 刀 knife at the bottom) + 刀 (another knife). Two knives crossing in front = scissors.
书 (book) + 包 (wrap/bag — a pictograph of something wrapped around an item). A bag for books = schoolbag.
Both characters share the 目 (eye) radical. 眼 = eye 目 + dwelling 艮 (where vision dwells). 睛 = eye 目 + clear 青. Clear eye-windows = eyes.

鼻 = 自 (self, which itself was originally a pictograph of a NOSE!) + 田 (field) + 廾 (two hands). Chinese speakers point to their own nose for 'me' — so 自 became 'self', and 鼻 was created to mean the actual nose.
耳 (ear — pictograph of an ear with its ridges) + 朵 (a measure word for flowers/blossoms, also pictures a hanging shape). Ears that hang like blossoms on the head.
嘴 = 口 (mouth) + 觜 (beak/snout). 巴 is a tail/handle suffix giving it a casual, familiar feel. The full 'mouth-thing' that you eat and speak with.
头 (head — two dots on top of 大 'big', like a head on a body) + 发 (emit/grow out). The strands that grow out of the head = hair.
月 (flesh, the same radical as in 肉/肚/脚) + 佥 (all/together). The 'flesh that gathers everything' (eyes, nose, mouth) — your face.
A pictograph of a hand: the curving lines are the fingers and palm. When 手 appears on the left side of compound characters, it becomes 扌(the 'hand radical' seen in 打, 拿, 把, 找).
月 (flesh) + 却 (step back/retreat). The fleshy part that steps — the foot.
月 (flesh) + 退 (retreat/move). The flesh that moves you forward and back — the leg.
肩 = 户 (door/hinge) + 月 (flesh) — the hinged joint where your arm swings like a door. 膀 = 月 (flesh) + 旁 (side) — the side flesh of the upper body.
北 (north — two people standing back-to-back) + 月 (flesh). The fleshy back-to-back side = your BACK. Also means 'to carry on the back' or 'recite from memory'.
月 (flesh) + 土 (earth/soil) — the fleshy 'soil' of the body where food gets digested. 子 is a familiar suffix.

牙 (tooth — pictograph of two interlocking molars/tusks) + 齿 (tooth — shows teeth inside an open mouth 凵, with 止 'stop' on top suggesting biting). Both characters mean tooth; together they emphasize teeth as a set.
心 (heart pictograph — the organ with chambers) + 脏 (organ — flesh 月 + 庄 farm/concealed). The flesh-organ of the heart. (心 alone is emotional 'heart'; 心脏 specifies the physical organ.)
紫 = 此 (this) + 糸 (silk) — 'THIS particular silk' was the rare purple silk worn only by emperors. + 色 (color). The royal silk color = purple.
橙 = 木 (tree) + 登 (climb/ascend) — climbing a tree to pick the orange fruit. + 色 (color). The orange-fruit color.
粉 = 米 (rice) + 分 (divide) — finely ground rice powder, like the pink face powder women used. + 色 (color) = pink.
灰 = 厂 (cliff/shelter) over 火 (fire) — ashes left in the hearth. + 色 (color). The color of ashes = gray.
春 = a stylized 'three' 三 + 人 (person) over 日 (sun) — three new shoots growing toward the sun. + 天 (day/sky). Spring days.
夏 originally depicted a person with a big head (页) and slow feet (夂) — dragging along under the summer heat. + 天 (day/sky). The slow, hot days of summer.
秋 = 禾 (grain) + 火 (fire). The season when grain ripens to a fiery golden color — autumn harvest. + 天 (day). Autumn days.
冬 = 夂 (slow walking) + 冫 (ice). Walking slowly on ice — the season of frozen ground. + 天 (day). Winter days.
暖 (warm — 日 sun + 爰 reaching/pulling, the sun's reaching warmth) + 和 (harmony — 禾 grain + 口 mouth). Sun-warmth in harmony = pleasantly warm.
凉 (cool — 冫 ice + 京 capital) + 快 (pleasant/fast — 忄 heart + 夬). An ice-pleasant feeling = refreshingly cool.
干 (dry — a flat stalk after harvest) + 燥 (parched — 火 fire + 喿 chirping birds in a tree, the dry hot summer). Fire-dried until parched.
潮 (tide — 氵 water + 朝 morning, the morning tide) + 湿 (wet — 氵 water + 显 reveal). Tidal moisture revealing itself in the air = humid.
海 (sea — 氵 water + 每 every, where every drop gathers) + 洋 (ocean — 氵 water + 羊 sheep, vast waves like flocks). The vast gathered waters = ocean.
森 (three 木 trees stacked — dense forest) + 林 (two 木 trees — woods). 5 trees together = a vast forest.
沙 (sand — 氵 water + 少 few, where water is scarce) + 漠 (vast/empty — 氵 + 莫 nothing). 'Water-scarce vast emptiness' = desert.
草 (grass — 艹 plant + 早 early) + 原 (plain/source — 厂 cliff + 泉 spring underneath). A grassy plain rolling from the cliffs = grassland.
风 (wind — an insect being swirled in a gust) + 景 (scene — 日 sun + 京 tall building, sunny view over the capital). Wind + sunny view = scenery.
橘 (orange tree — 木 wood + 矞 phonetic) + 子 (fruit/seed suffix). The orange fruit.
草 (grass) + 莓 (berry — 艹 plant + 每 every, every cluster a berry). A berry that grows on plants close to the grass = strawberry.
利 (sharp/profit — 禾 grain + 刂 knife) + 木 (tree). The tree whose 'profit' is its juicy fruit = pear tree.
桃 (peach — 木 tree + 兆 omen, peach blossoms are a lucky omen) + 子 (fruit/seed suffix). The peach fruit.
菠 (艹 plant + 波 wave) + 萝 (艹 plant + 罗 net) — both have the plant radical 艹. A spiky, netted plant from across the waves = pineapple.
白 (white) + 菜 (vegetable — 艹 plant + 采 pluck). The white-stemmed leafy vegetable = bok choy / Chinese cabbage.
西 (western) + 红 (red) + 柿 (persimmon — 木 tree + 市 market). The 'Western red persimmon' (imported from the West) = tomato.
黄 (yellow) + 瓜 (melon — a pictograph of a vine with a hanging fruit). Cucumbers turn yellow when fully ripe, so they're called 'yellow melons'.
土 (earth) + 豆 (bean — a pictograph of a stemmed vessel originally for offerings, repurposed for legumes). An 'earth-bean' that grows underground = potato.
胡 (foreign — used as a prefix for things imported from Central Asia) + 萝卜 (radish — 艹 plant on both). The 'foreign radish' brought along the Silk Road = carrot.
洋 (overseas/foreign — 氵 water + 羊) + 葱 (scallion — 艹 plant + 总). The foreign/overseas scallion = onion (introduced from abroad).
大 (big) + 蒜 (garlic — 艹 plant + 祘 'two altars' / calculate). The 'big plant of many cloves' = garlic.
Both characters have 艹 (plant): 蘑 = 艹 + 磨 grind (a plant ground into sauce); 菇 = 艹 + 姑 aunt. Together = mushroom.
海 (sea) + 鲜 (fresh — 鱼 fish + 羊 sheep, the two freshest foods). Fresh from the sea = seafood.
龙 (dragon) + 虾 (shrimp — 虫 reptile/crustacean + 下). The 'dragon shrimp' with its long claws and armored body = lobster.
Both characters share 虫 (insect/crustacean): 螃 = 虫 + 旁 side (the side-walker); 蟹 = 虫 + 解 separate/cut apart (with pinching claws). The sideways-walking pincher = crab.
可 (can/ok) + 乐 (joy/pleasure). 'Can-be-happy' — a clever transliteration of 'Cola' that also sounds joyful in Chinese.
啤 (口 mouth + 卑 humble — phonetic transliteration of 'beer' sound) + 酒 (alcohol — 氵 water + 酉 wine jar). A frothy mouth-pleasing alcohol = beer.
白 (white/clear) + 酒 (alcohol — 氵 water + 酉 wine jar). Clear strong Chinese spirit distilled from sorghum = baijiu.
购 (purchase — 贝 shell-money + 勾 hook) + 物 (thing — 牛 ox + 勿). To 'hook things with money' = shopping.
打 (hit/do — 扌 hand + 丁 nail) + 折 (break — 扌 hand + 斤 axe). To 'hit a break' off the price = a discount.
刷 (brush/swipe — 尸 figure + 巾 cloth + 刂 knife, brushing motion) + 卡 (card — 上 + 下 stuck between, the original meaning was 'stuck'). To swipe the stuck card = pay by card.
现 (present/now — 王 jade + 见 see) + 金 (gold/money). Money you can see in the present moment = cash.
发 (issue/emit) + 票 (ticket — 西 cover + 示 reveal, an official slip). An issued ticket = a receipt or invoice.
考 (examine — an elder 老 with a stick, testing) + 试 (try — 讠 speech + 式 form). To examine and try = an exam.
作 (do/make — 亻 person + 乍 suddenly) + 业 (work/business — a pictograph of a wooden rack for hanging bells). Work that you do for class = homework.
操 (drill/exercise — 扌 hand + 喿 birds chirping in tree, lots of activity) + 场 (field — 土 earth + 昜 sun rays). The exercise-field = playground or sports yard.
图 (diagram/picture — 囗 enclosure + 冬) + 书 (book) + 馆 (hall — 饣 + 官 official). The hall of picture-books = library.
毕 (finish — 比 compare + 十, finishing the comparison) + 业 (studies/work). Finishing one's studies = to graduate.
面 (face) + 试 (try/test — 讠 speech + 式 form). A face-to-face test = interview.
加 (add — 力 strength + 口 mouth) + 班 (shift). Adding an extra shift = overtime work.
退 (retreat — 辶 walk + 艮 stop) + 休 (rest — 亻 person + 木 tree). Retreating from work to rest = to retire.
压 (press down — 厂 cliff + 土 earth, the weight of a cliff pressing on earth) + 力 (force/strength). A pressing force = pressure.
努 (exert — 奴 servant + 力 strength, a servant's hard labor) + 力 (force). Exerting all your strength = making effort.
公 (public — 八 split fairly + 厶) + 交 (exchange — 亠 + 父) + 车 (vehicle). The public exchange-vehicle that everyone rides = bus.
地 (ground/earth) + 铁 (iron — 钅 metal + 失). The underground iron-track train = subway/metro.
自 (self) + 行 (move/go) + 车 (vehicle). The self-propelled vehicle = bicycle.
司 (manage/take charge — a kneeling figure issuing orders) + 机 (machine — short for 飞机/汽车). The one who manages the vehicle = driver.
堵 (block — 土 earth/wall + 者 person, a wall of people) + 车 (cars). A wall of blocked cars = traffic jam.
视 (sight — 礻 spirit/altar + 见 see) + 频 (frequency — 步 step + 页 head, repeated head-motions). Rapid sight-frames = video.
账 (account — 贝 shell-money + 长 long, long money records) + 号 (number — 口 mouth + 丂). An account number.
发 (emit/send out) + 送 (deliver — 辶 walk + 关 close). Both verbs for sending — to dispatch or send.
机 (short for 飞机 plane) + 票 (ticket — 西 cover + 示 reveal). A plane ticket.
景 (scene — 日 sun + 京 tall building) + 点 (dot/spot — 占 + 灬). A scenic spot.
导 (guide/lead — 巳 + 寸 inch/hand, leading by the hand) + 游 (roam — 氵 water + 㪺). The leader of roaming visitors = tour guide.
护 (protect — 扌 hand + 户 door, guarding the door) + 照 (shine/photograph — 召 + 灬 fire/light). A protective document with your photograph = passport.
签 (sign — ⺮ bamboo + 佥 all together, an inked bamboo seal) + 证 (proof — 讠 speech + 正 correct). Signed proof to enter a country = visa.
行 (travel/move) + 李 (plum — 木 tree + 子 child; also a common surname/load). Your traveling 'load' = luggage.
纪 (record — 纟 silk + 己 self) + 念 (remember — 今 today + 心 heart) + 品 (article — three 口 stacked, items). An article to remember by = souvenir.
羽 (feather — pictograph of two wings) + 毛 (hair/feather, a tuft) + 球 (ball). A feathered ball = badminton shuttlecock.
乒 + 乓 are 兵 (soldier) with one stroke removed on each side — these characters literally mimic the 'ping-pong' sound. + 球 (ball). Onomatopoetic = ping-pong.
瑜 (jade luster — 王 jade + 俞) + 伽 (亻 person + 加, Sanskrit suffix). A phonetic transliteration of Sanskrit 'yoga'.
健 (healthy — 亻 person + 建 build) + 身 (body — pictograph of a pregnant figure) + 房 (room). The body-building room = gym.
自 (self — originally a nose) + 信 (believe — 亻 person + 言 speech). To believe in oneself = confidence.
轻 (light/easy — 车 cart + 圣 skillful) + 松 (loose/pine — 木 tree + 公). 'Light and loose' like swaying pine branches = relaxed.
The cold north (北) where people sit back-to-back for warmth in that direction (方).
Warm southern regions (南) with lush hanging vegetation in that direction (方).
The direction (方) where the sun rises behind the trees (东).
The direction (方) where the bird returns to its square nest (西) at sunset.
At the corner of a square side (旁) and the edge of a path (边).
In the gap (间) that strikes directly through the center (中).
A small meeting (会) of time that passes like a child (儿).
Reaching the end thread (终) at (于) last.
A unique cow (特) that is separated (别) from the herd.
A banner (常) that is constantly (经) waving in the wind.
The reason (因) for (为) the action.
If (如) you have the fruit (果), you can share it.
比 (compare — two people back-to-back) + 较 (compare — 车 cart + 交 cross). Comparing two things — to compare, or used as 'relatively/fairly'.
The total (总) situation is (是) always true.
The certain (必) need for the whiskers/details (须).
Having the ability (能) for it to be true (可).
What you ought (应) to deserve (该).
The solid truth (实) of that (其) matter.
The source (原) and intent (意) of the heart.
A heavy (重) want (要) or necessity.
A simple list (单) written on bamboo (⺮).
Many items (复) mixed together (杂).
A woman under a roof (安) is safe and whole (全).
Perched on a cliff (危) in a steep area (险).
Trapped (困) trying to catch a bird (难).
A convenient (便) price that is appropriate (宜) for your wallet.
The value (价) set in the grid (格).
The surrounding ring (环) of the border (境).
New (新) fish and sheep (鲜) are fresh.
Dry (干) and pure (净) as mountain water.
Wholly (整) even (齐) and orderly.
Being able to stretch (舒) out in your clothes (服).
A full (满) intent (意) of the heart.
Touching (着) an urgent (急) spot.
Carrying (担) the heart (心) in anxiety.
Releasing (放) the heart (心) from worry.
Keeping a small (小) focused heart (心).
Opening (开) the heart (心) to joy.
A fast (快) surge of joy and music (乐) in the heart.
The blessing (福) of good fortune (幸).
Born/Rising (生) spirit/air (气).
A wounded (伤) heart (心).
Hard (难) to pass (过) the time.
Suffering harm (害) and letting the heart tremble with fear (怕).
Daring (敢) with strength (勇).
Sharp ears/mind (聪) and bright (明) eyes.
As dull as a bamboo (⺮) root (本).
Able (可) to be loved (爱).
Bright (亮) as if floating (漂) in the clear light of day.
丿 + 巾 (cloth/banner). A sharp banner-bearer — handsome, dashing, sharp-looking. (In traditional 帥, the full general's banner.)
A pictograph: top depicts long flowing hair (耂) + 匕 (a bent figure leaning on a cane). An elderly person with long hair stooped over a cane — OLD.
A person who is light (轻) in years (年).
Meat (月) that is cut in large half (半) portions.
疒 (sickness radical, a person leaning on a bed) + 叟 (old man). A sickly old man — wasted and THIN.
A tall multi-story tower with a pointed roof reaching for the sky.
矢 (arrow — short and quick) + 委 (entrust/yield). Short as a tucked-away arrow — short in height.
Originally a pictograph of an elderly person with very long flowing hair and a cane. Means 'long' (cháng) and also 'grow up / elder' (pronounced zhǎng).
矢 (arrow — short shaft) + 豆 (bean — a small thing on a stand). Short as a stubby arrow next to a bean — SHORT in length.
A heart (忄) that makes quick decisions and acts fast.
A heart (忄) that takes a long time under the sun (日) to decide.
亲 (relative/close, on the left) + 斤 (axe). Taking an axe to the trees to clear a new path — fresh, NEW.
丨 (vertical) + 日 (sun). The sun seen in passing days gone by — old (of objects). Contrasts with 老 (old people) and 古 (ancient).
Your right hand (又) pointing accurately toward the target (寸/measure).
钅 (metal) + 昔 (former/past). Misaligned metalwork from older times — an error, wrong.
Walking (辶) toward a long distance with a robe tucked in.
Walking (辶) within the strike of an axe (斤).
Two evenings (夕) stacked. Many nights have passed, representing a large amount.
Something already small (小) with an extra cut, making it even fewer or lesser.
A person (人) stretching their arms wide to show just how big something is.
A person with arms held tight against their body, trying to occupy as little space as possible.
A woman (女) with her child (子) is considered the embodiment of goodness and harmony.
Spoiled soil (土) and negation.
Fires (灬) burning under a platform where things are being handled (执). Very HOT.
Ice (冫) and a command (令) to shiver. It is freezing cold.
Heart (忄) dying (亡) from too much work.
Working the field (田) with thread (糸) until exhaustion.
A tree trapped in a box. A tired heart seeking rest.
Trying to catch a bird (隹) in the sticky clay (堇).
Simple enough to contain (容) in a small box (易).
Food (饣) wrapped (包) up inside the stomach.
When the ego (我) demands food (饣).
When the mouth seeks water (氵) under the hot sun.
sentence_en: I want to drink water, I'm thirsty.

mnemonic: 疒 (sickness radical) + 冬 (winter). The aches you get in the cold of winter — illness pain.
Strong (健) and flourishing (康) state.
Born (生) into a sickness (病).
A situation that is fresh (刚) and has only just (才) occurred.
Using the front (前) part of the timeline to indicate things in the past.
The time that follows with (以) the back (后) of events.
The time that is most (最) near (近) to the present moment.
The path (经) is already finished (已).
Although (虽) it seems like insects, it is still natural (然).
But (但) it is true (是) that things changed.
Because of that place (所) with (以) those conditions.
口 (mouth) + 乞 (beg). The mouth begs for food — to eat.
口 (mouth) + 曷 (shouting figure). You shout 'I'm thirsty!' and bring liquid to the mouth — drink.
A pictograph of a hand holding an axe or staff — the authority figure of the household. The formal word for father.
A pictograph derived from 女 (woman) with two extra dots indicating breasts — a nursing mother. The formal word for mother.
田 (field) + 力 (strength/power). The strong one who works the fields — a man.
子 (child) + 亥 (a phonetic component, also the last earthly branch). The little child. Usually used as 孩子.
矢 (arrow) + 口 (mouth). Your words fly out as straight and sure as an arrow — you KNOW what you're talking about.
辶 (walk) + 首 (head). Walking with your head held high down the road — the way. Also 'the Dao' in Daoism.
宀 (roof) + 子 (child). A child born under a scholar's roof learns characters. The character itself.
A pictograph of a person with a tattoo on their chest — the original 'cultural marking'. Now means literature, text, and culture.
讠 (speech radical) + 吾 (I/me). My words, my language.
讠 (speech) + 舌 (tongue). The words your tongue produces — speech.
夕 (evening) + 口 (mouth). In the dark evening you can't see — so you have to call out your NAME with your mouth.
讠 (speech) + 果 (fruit). Words that bear fruit — a lesson taught and learned.
亻 (person) + 言 (speech). When a person stands by their words, you can BELIEVE them. Also means a written letter.
扌 (hand) + 卩 (kneel) + 又 (hand). Hands delivering news to the kneeling official — a report or newspaper.
讠 (speech) + 井 (well). Words drawn up from deep within, like water from a well — to explain or lecture.
忄 (heart) + 董 (direct/manage). When your heart grasps and manages the idea — you understand.
孝 (filial piety/child) + 攵 (tap with a stick). A child being gently tapped by the teacher's stick — to teach.
学 (study, top portion) + 见 (see). When you study and see, you sense or feel the answer. Also pronounced jiào in 睡觉 (sleep).
A simplified pictograph of a horse — you can almost see the mane on top, the body in the middle, and the four legs (now a single sweep) at the bottom.
A pictograph of a bird in profile — head with eye (the dot), body, and tail. Easy to spot once you see it.
A pictograph: head on top, body with scales (田-like middle), and a tail-fin (一) at the bottom. Originally drawn vertically as a fish.
A pictograph of a cow's head seen head-on — the slanted top stroke is one horn, the vertical line is the face, the crossing strokes are the other horn and ears.
A pictograph of a sheep's face seen head-on — the two top strokes are curved horns, the rest is the face. Looks 'fluffy' compared to 牛.
犭 (dog/animal radical) + 者 (person/thing). The animal that 'is what it is' — the pig.
犭 (dog/animal radical, a stylized dog on its hind legs) + 句 (phonetic). The dog.
犭 (animal) + 苗 (sprout — grass 艹 over field 田). The animal that prowls among sprouts in the field — a cat hunting mice.
又 (hand) + 鸟 (bird). The bird you can catch by hand — the domestic chicken.
甲 (first/armor) + 鸟 (bird). The 'armored' bird with a beak and webbed feet — the duck.
A pictograph of a coiled worm or insect. This is the radical used in many insect-related characters.
A simplified form of a much more complex traditional 龍. Imagine a stylized dragon with a horned head (top stroke) and a coiling body. Symbol of China.
艹 (grass/plant) + 化 (change/transform). Plants that transform into beautiful blossoms — flowers.
艹 (grass/plant) + 早 (early/morning). The morning grass that grows everywhere — common grass.
木 + 木 = two trees side by side = a wood. (And 森 is three trees stacked, a dense forest.)
厂 (cliff) + 口 (a stone). A stone fallen from a cliff. The 口 here represents a rock, not a mouth.
氵 (water) + 可 (can/may). The water that can flow on and on — a river.
氵 (water) + 工 (work). A large river — the kind ancient people had to WORK with for transport and farming. (Often refers to the Yangtze.)
氵 (water) + 每 (every). Where every drop of water gathers — the SEA.
氵 (water) + 胡 (whiskers/old man). Calm water with reedy 'whiskers' along the shore — a lake.
冫 (ice radical, two ice crystals) + 水 (water). Frozen water = ice. The 冫 radical itself means 'ice' and shows up in 冷 and 凉.
Looks like a roof 人 over 王 (king) with two dots. The king's treasure hidden in the earth = precious metal/gold. The radical 钅is its compact form.
钅(metal) + 艮 (steady/dwelling). Steady, glimmering metal = silver.
钅(metal) + 失 (lose). The metal you don't want to LOSE in battle — iron for swords and tools.
王 (king) with an extra dot. The king's most precious gemstone — jade. (王 itself comes from this without the dot.)
钅(metal) + 中 (center). A metal object struck at the center to mark hours — a bell or clock.
女 (woman) + 台 (platform/foundation). It begins with a woman — every life on earth has its foundation in a mother. 'Begin'.
宀 (roof) + 元 (origin/first). Back under the roof where you started — the task is FINISHED.
Originally a pictograph of a field densely planted in a circle. A complete cycle = a week. Also the Zhou dynasty surname.
禾 (grain) + 子 (child). The young grain marking the time of year — a season.
⺮ (bamboo) + 弟 (younger brother). Brothers ranked in order like bamboo segments — used to make ordinals: 第一 (first), 第二 (second).
冫 (ice/two-dots) + 欠 (yawn/owe). A second yawn, a second go — a counting of times.
A figure of a person bent over from waiting a LONG time. The character looks like someone slowly aging.
Derived from 水 (water) — water flows forever. The character contains all eight basic brush strokes; calligraphers practice it as the 'eternal eight strokes'.
户 (door) + 方 (square). A square space behind a door — a room.
木 (wood) + 娄 (drag/heap). A heap of wood stacked into many stories — a multi-story building.
穴 (cave/hole) + 囱 (chimney). A hole in the wall for light, like a chimney for the eyes — a window.
阝 (hill/wall) + 完 (complete). A walled-off enclosure — a courtyard or institution like 医院 (hospital).
囗 (enclosure) + 元 (origin). An enclosed plot of land where plants originate — a garden.
冂 (boundary/walls) + 人 (person). A person inside the walls — inside.
夕 (evening) + 卜 (divination). In the evening, oracles were consulted OUTSIDE the home. = outside.
辶 (walk) + 力 (strength). Walking with strength along the edge — the side/border.
广 (a roof/shelter shape) + 木 (wood). Wood under a shelter — a wooden bed at home.
火 (fire) + 丁 (nail/peg). A flame on a peg — a lamp.
Two dots (eyes/hair) on top of 大 (big) — a big head with features. The main thing on top = head, leader.
A pictograph of two interlocking molars or tusks. Compare with 齿 (which also means tooth, showing teeth in a mouth).
皿 (dish/vessel) with a drop on top. A drop of liquid in a sacrificial vessel — blood.
Top is a stylized bone joint; bottom is 月 (flesh). Bone within flesh — bone.
A hand (又) pulling off a hide. Skin or leather peeled from an animal.
口 (mouth) + 觜 (beak/horn). The 'beak' part of you that eats and speaks — your mouth. More physical than 口 alone.
千 (thousand) + 口 (mouth). The thing that does a thousand tricks in the mouth — the tongue.
⺮ (bamboo) + 夭 (tilted). Bamboo bending in the wind looks like it's laughing. Or: tilt your head back when laughing.
Two 口 (mouths) over 大 (a big crying person) — wailing out loud with two open mouths and tears.
喜 (joyous — drum 壴 + mouth 口, beating drums and singing) + 欢 (cheer — 又 hand + 欠 yawning/breath). To like = to feel joy and cheer.
Originally had 心 (heart) at the center. Now: a hand 爫 reaching down to a friend 友. Reaching down with affection — love.
立 (stand upright) + 占 (occupy a spot). To stand and occupy a position. Also means 'station' (where you stand to wait).
王 (jade-like, but here a precious bead) + 元 (origin). Playing with a precious bead — to play with toys, to have fun.
扌 (hand) + 戈 (halberd/weapon). Hand wielding a weapon while searching — to seek out.
合 (combine/together) + 手 (hand). Combining the hand around an object — to grasp and TAKE it.
方 (square direction) + 攵 (tap with stick). Tapping something to send it off in a direction — to release or put down.
田 (field — originally 囟 the brain/skull) + 心 (heart). Brain and heart working together — to think.
亦 (also) + 又 (hand). The hand makes something also be different — to change/transform.
亻 (person upright) + 匕 (person upside-down). One person flipping to the other position — transformation.
足 (foot) + 包 (wrap). Feet wrapping around the ground in quick succession — to run.
A pictograph of a bird with wings spread, swooping. Look at the upward curve and the dot — it's almost a flying bird.
口 (mouth) + 昌 (prosper/bright, two suns). Two suns of joy coming out of the mouth — singing.
足 (foot) + 兆 (omen/trillion). Feet leaping into the air at a sign — to jump.
氵 (water) + 㪺 (banner/child). A child holding a banner in the water — wandering/swimming.
目 (eye) + 垂 (drop down/hang). The eyes droop down — falling asleep.
酉 (wine jar) + 星 (star). Waking up under the morning star after a night of wine — sobering, waking up.
走 (walk/run) + 己 (oneself). To get oneself up and moving — rise.
歹 (bone fragments) + 匕 (a fallen figure). A person fallen beside bones — death.
氵 (water) + 舌 (tongue). Water + working tongue = a living person who can drink and speak.
邦 (state/country) + 巾 (cloth). The community uses its resources (cloth) to help — to assist.
且 (additionally) + 力 (strength). Adding extra strength — to assist.
辶 (walking) + 关 (close/related). Walking with someone to see them off, or sending a gift — to send/give.
扌 (hand) + 妾 (concubine — 立 stand + 女 woman). Hand reaching out to greet someone — to receive/welcome.
丩 (bind/gather) + 攵 (tap). Tapping things together to gather them up — to collect/receive.
Imagine an arrow being released from a bow — emitting outward. To send out or emit.
耳 (ear) + 又 (hand). In ancient warfare, the victor took the ear of the defeated — to take/fetch.
亻 (person) + 昔 (former/past time). A person from before who lent you something — borrow/lend.
A perfectly horizontal line with stable supports — flat, level, peaceful.
宀 (roof) + 女 (woman). A woman safely under the roof of home — peaceful, safe.
青 (blue/clear) + 争 (contend). When the contending stops and only clarity remains — quiet.
弓 (bow) + 虽 (although) — a tough drawn bow plus an insect 虫. Even a tiny insect can be powerful in numbers; the drawn bow is full of force. Strong.
Two 弓 (bows) with feathers/decorations — bows that have lost their strength and only have decorative feathers. Weak.
冂 (frame) + 一 + 口 (mouth). All the mouths under one frame are saying the SAME thing.
人 (person/roof) + 王 (king/jade). Everything under one ruler — the whole, complete.
A flame on a candle stand — the central flame of the household. The master/main thing.
亻 (person) + 叚 (borrow). A person who BORROWS another's identity — fake. (Also pronounced jià meaning 'vacation/leave'.)
羊 (sheep) + 大 (big). A big, fat sheep was the most beautiful sight to ancient herders — symbol of beauty.
禾 (grain) + 日 (sun/originally 甘 sweet). Grain ripening in the sun gives off a sweet fragrance.
A person 千 carrying a heavy load 里 on their back — weighed down, heavy. Also means 'important' (carrying weight).
车 (vehicle) + 圣 (skillful). A cart that's easy to push — light.
里 (village) + 灬 (fire). A village blackened by soot from many fires — black.
Like a sun (日) with a slanting ray on top — the pale dawn light. White, blank, pure.
纟 (silk) + 工 (work). The hard work of dyeing silk red — the prized red silk color.
纟 (silk) + 录 (record). Silk recorded as the color of fresh leaves — green.
艹 (plant) + 监 (oversee). The indigo plant overseen for dyeing — blue dye, blue color.
Originally a pictograph of a person wearing a jade ornament. The color of jade and ripe grain — yellow.
A pictograph of something curled and wrapping around a baby (巳) — to wrap up. Also means a bag (something that wraps things).
土 (earth) + 皿 (dish). Salt 'earth' on a dish — sea salt or rock salt prepared for the table.
米 (rice) + 唐 (Tang dynasty). The Tang dynasty perfected refining sweet rice into crystals — sugar.
氵 (water) + 由 (from/cause). A 'water' that flows but isn't water — oil.
氵 (water) + 昜 (sun rays/warmth). Hot water with sun-like warmth steaming from the bowl — soup.
面 (flour/noodles) + 包 (wrap). Flour wrapped into a loaf — bread.
牛 (cow) + 奶 (milk/breast — 女 woman + 乃 'nai' phonetic). Cow milk.
女 (woman) + 乃 (phonetic 'nai'). What a woman provides — milk. Also used in 奶奶 (grandma).
石 (stone) + 宛 (curved). A curved stone or ceramic vessel — a bowl.
般 (sort/general) + 皿 (dish). A general flat dish — a plate or tray.
⺮ (bamboo) + 快 (fast). Fast bamboo sticks for eating — chopsticks.
A pictograph of a curved blade with a handle. The 刂 form on the right side of characters (like 别, 刚) is its compact version.
又 (hand) with a dot in the crook. A hand making an X / fork shape.
A simplified pictograph: top-down view of a chariot with two wheels (the horizontal lines) joined by an axle (vertical) and a yoke. Any wheeled vehicle.
舟 (boat — pictograph of a boat from above) + 㕣 (river valley). A boat in the valley waters — a ship.
报 (report) + 纸 (paper — 纟silk + 氏 clan). Reports printed on paper — newspaper.
钥 (lock — 钅metal + 月) + 匙 (spoon-like instrument). A metal spoon-like tool to unlock things — a key.
雨 (rain) + 伞 (a pictograph of an umbrella — a domed top with ribs and a handle). A rain umbrella.
钅 (metal) + 竟 (finally/completely). A polished metal surface that finally reflects everything — a mirror.
疒 (sickness radical, a person leaning on a bed) + 丙 (the third in heavenly stems). A person lying sick in bed — illness.
疒 (sickness) + 甬 (passageway). Pain shooting through the body's passages.
此 (this) + 二 (two). 'These two-or-so' — meaning 'some, a few'. Used as 一些 'some'.
A simple pictograph of a small table or stool — counting a few items on it. Asks 'how many?' or means 'a few'.
夂 (slow steps coming down) + 口 (mouth). Each person walks up and speaks their own piece — each one separately.
Top is 'person/hair' and bottom is 母 (mother). Every person has a mother — every, each.
走 (walk) + 戉 (axe). Walking past with an axe — exceeding limits. Used in 越来越 'more and more'.
木 (tree) + 及 (reach). Reaching the very top of the tree — the extreme limit.
非 (not — two wings facing opposite ways) + 常 (usual). 'Not usual' = extraordinary, very.
A pictograph of a sprout just breaking through soil — JUST now appearing. Used to mean 'only just' or 'not until then'.
自 (self — originally a nose, since Chinese point to their nose to mean 'me') + 己 (oneself — a coiling cord, a personal self). 'Oneself'.
Originally a pictograph of a NOSE. Chinese speakers point to their own nose for 'me', so 自 came to mean 'self'. (The new character for nose is 鼻.)
Looks like 己 (self) but with the top closed off — the action has already been sealed. Already done.
教 (teach) + 室 (room — 宀 roof + 至 arrive). The room where teaching happens — classroom.
字 (character) + 典 (canon/classic book — books 册 on a stand 丌). A canonical book of characters — dictionary.
数 (count/number — 攵 tap + 娄 string) + 学 (study). Studying numbers — mathematics.
历 (experience/pass through) + 史 (record — a hand holding a brush over records). Records of what has been passed through — history.
周 (week/circle) + 末 (end — 木 tree with a top mark showing the end of branches). The end of the week — weekend.
⺮ (bamboo) + 卩 (kneeling figure). Bamboo divided into segments at the joints — sections; also festivals (set sections of the year).
行 (go/walk, split around) + 圭 (jade tablets, stacked). Walking down a street paved with jade-like stones — a street.
楼 (building) + 梯 (ladder — 木 wood + 弟 younger brother in succession). The wooden ladder/steps of a building — stairs.
电 (electric) + 梯 (ladder). An electric ladder — elevator.
机 (machine — short for 飞机 plane) + 场 (field, 土 earth + 昜 sun rays). A field for planes — airport.
车 (vehicle) + 站 (stand/station). Where vehicles stand — a station.
餐 (meal — top: bone/eating + 食 food) + 厅 (hall — 厂 shelter + 丁 nail). A hall for meals — restaurant.
雨 (rain) + 令 (command). A command to count even the tiniest raindrop — zero, the very smallest. (Also means 'fragmentary'.)
亻 (person) + 咅 (split/sprout). People doubling in number — multiplied, several times.
一 (one) + 起 (rise/get up). All rising as one — together.
一 (one) + 定 (fixed — 宀 roof + 正 right). Fixed at one outcome — definitely, for sure.
一 (one) + 样 (kind/sample — 木 wood + 羊 sheep). Of one kind — the same.
一 (one) + 直 (straight — 十 cross + 目 eye + 一 line). Going one way straight through — continuously, always.
知 (know — arrow 矢 + mouth 口, speaking as sure as an arrow) + 道 (the way). To know the way — to know.
觉 (sense — see + study) + 得 (obtain). To 'get' a sense of something — to feel that, to think that.
讨 (demand — 讠speech + 寸 inch) + 厌 (hate). Words that demand and bother — annoying, disliked.
关 (close/connect) + 心 (heart). Closely connected hearts — to care for someone.
感 (feel — 咸 all + 心 heart, the heart fully responding) + 觉 (sense). A full sensing — a feeling.
心 (heart) + 情 (emotion — 忄heart + 青 blue/clear). The state of the heart — mood.
情 (emotion/circumstance) + 况 (condition — 冫 ice/cold + 兄 brother). The circumstantial condition — situation.
忄 (heart) + 白 (white/blank). The heart goes white/blank with fear.
刍 (catching a hand) + 心 (heart). The heart caught in a rush — urgent, in a hurry.
然 (so/naturally — 月 + 犬 dog + 灬 fire, naturally things happen) + 后 (after). 'So afterwards' — then, next.
当 (should/at the time) + 然 (so/naturally). Naturally, as it should be — of course.
突 (dash/protrude — 穴 hole + 犬 dog, a dog dashing out of a hole) + 然 (so). Suddenly, like a dog leaping out.
其 (that) + 他 (he/it). 'That other one' — other things, others.
另 (other — 口 mouth + 力 strength, calling out separately) + 外 (outside). The other one outside this — additional.
自 (self) + 然 (so/thus). Being its own way — natural, nature.
爪 (claw) + 巴 (cling). Clinging with claws — climbing.
扌 (hand) + 隹 (bird). A hand pushing a bird to take flight — to push.
扌 (hand) + 立 (stand). A hand pulling something to make it stand — to pull.
车 (vehicle) + 欠 (yawn/lack). A vehicle that lacks rigidity — sags, soft.
石 (stone) + 更 (more). Hard as stone — hard.
囗 (enclosure) + 员 (member). Members gathered in a circle — round.
A pictograph of a plow turning the earth in straight squared rows — a square shape and direction.
十 (ten) + 目 (eye) + 一 (line). Ten eyes looking along one line to see it's straight.
亦 + 弓 (bow). A bent bow — curve/bend.
起 (rise) + 来 (come). Rise and come — to get up. Also a directional complement: 'up'.
下 (down) + 来 (come). Come down.
上 (up) + 来 (come). Come up.
出 (out) + 来 (come). Come out.
进 (enter) + 去 (go). Enter and go in.
奶 (milk/grandmother — 女 woman + 乃) doubled. The kindly milk-giver of the family — paternal grandmother.
爷 (paternal grandpa — 父 father + 卩 figure). Father's father — paternal grandpa.
外 (outside/other side) + 公 (elderly man). The grandfather on the 'outside' (mother's side) — maternal grandpa.
外 (outside) + 婆 (old woman — 波 wave + 女 woman). The old woman of the 'outside' family — maternal grandma.
阿 (prefix of familiarity) + 姨 (mother's sister — 女 woman + 夷 phonetic). A friendly term for aunt (mother's sister), and used to address any middle-aged woman politely.
叔 (uncle — 上 + 小 small + 又 hand). Father's younger brother. Also used for any friendly middle-aged man.
A pictograph of a rabbit: long ears (top), body and tail (the dot). Look closely — you'll see the bunny shape.
A pictograph of a rodent: top is the head with teeth, bottom is the body and tail. The first zodiac animal.
虫 (insect/reptile) + 它 (it). The 'creeping it' — snake.
能 (able/capable) + 灬 (fire/feet). The mighty 'able' beast — bear.
A pictograph of an elephant — you can see the trunk at the top curving down. Also means 'image, resemble'.
礻 (altar/spirit radical, a form of 示 'show/reveal') + 申 (state/declare). Spirits revealing themselves — god.
A pictograph of a creature with a big strange head — a ghost or spirit of the dead.
大 (big) + 学 (study). The 'big' school — university.
中 (middle) + 学 (study). Middle school.
小 (small) + 学 (study). Small/elementary school.
节 (festival/section) + 日 (day). A festival day.
假 (here pronounced jià: leave/holiday) + 期 (period). A period of leave — vacation.
邻 (neighboring — 阝city/wall + 令 ordering) + 居 (dwell — 尸 body + 古 old). One whose dwelling is next to yours — neighbor.
客 (guest — 宀 roof + 各 each, each one welcomed under a roof) + 人 (person). A person who is a guest.
大 (big) + 人 (person). A 'big person' — adult.
孩 (child) + 子 (suffix). A child.
老 (old — a stooped elder with a cane) + 人 (person). Old person.
宀 (roof) + 一 + 口 (mouth) + 田 (field). A home with a mouth fed by fields — wealthy/rich.
穴 (cave) + 力 (force). Forced to live in a cave — poor.
你 (you) + 心 (heart). 'You with my heart' — the respectful, formal form of 'you' used for elders, teachers, customers.
木 (tree) with a mark at the bottom showing the ROOT. Means origin/root, and is the measure word for books (since books were once made from tree bark).
公 (public/standard) + 里 (a Chinese unit of distance, originally ~500m). The 'public li' = the standardized international kilometer.
夂 (slow) + 木 (tree branch). A long branch — measure word for long thin things: rivers (一条河), roads (一条路), fish (一条鱼).
口 (mouth) + 阿 (phonetic 'a'). A mouth uttering 'ah!' — exclamation or softening particle at the end of a sentence.
木 (wood) + 对 (right/face). A tall standing-upright tree. (Distinct from 木 which is generic 'wood/tree'.)
口 (mouth) + 十 (ten). Many 'tongues' growing out — leaves of a tree.
果 (fruit) + 树 (tree). A fruit-bearing tree.
瓶 (bottle — 并 together + 瓦 tile/clay). A clay vessel — bottle. + 子 suffix.
盒 (box — 合 close/join + 皿 dish). A closing dish/box. + 子 suffix.
袋 (bag — 代 substitute/take place of + 衣 clothing). A cloth substitute for hands — a bag.
钱 (money) + 包 (bag). Money bag — wallet.
⺮ (bamboo) + 毛 (fur/hair). A bamboo handle with hair at the end — a brush, and now any writing pen.
纟 (silk) + 氏 (clan). Originally silk-based paper invented for clans/records.
故 (old/reason — 古 ancient + 攵 tap) + 事 (affair). Old affairs told — a story.
音 (sound — 立 stand + 日 sun, sound rising up) + 乐 (joy/music). Joyful sounds — music.
哥 (older brother — two 可) + 欠 (yawn/open mouth). An older brother's open mouth filling the air — singing a song.
Originally a pictograph of a dancer with sleeves flowing on both sides and feet (舛) below. Dancing with flowing sleeves.
电视 (TV) + 剧 (drama — 居 dwelling + 刂 knife). A TV drama series.
新 (new) + 闻 (hear — 门 door + 耳 ear, an ear at the door). Newly heard — news.
记 (record — 讠speech + 己 oneself) + 者 (person). The person who records — reporter.
作 (make/create — 亻person + 乍 suddenly) + 家 (expert/home). One whose 'home' is creating — a writer.
艺 (skill — 艹 plant + 乙 talent) + 术 (technique). Skilled technique — art.
A pictograph of a hand drawing the boundaries of a field — to paint or draw. Also means 'a painting'.
照 (shine/illuminate — 召 + 灬 fire) + 片 (slice/sheet). Light captured on a sheet — a photograph.
相 (mutual/image — 木 tree + 目 eye) + 机 (machine). The image-machine — camera.
游 (roam/play) + 戏 (play/drama). Playing around — game.
礼 (rite/etiquette — 礻altar + 乚) + 物 (thing — 牛 ox + 勿). A ceremonial thing — gift.
礻 (altar/spirit radical) + 兄 (elder brother). The elder at the altar offering blessings — to wish someone well.
请 (invite) + 客 (guest). To invite as a guest — to treat someone (especially to a meal).
结 (tie a knot — 纟silk + 吉 lucky) + 婚 (marry — 女 woman + 昏 dusk, evening weddings). Tying the lucky knot — to marry.
出 (come out) + 生 (birth/grow). Coming out into life — to be born.
长 (grow — here pronounced zhǎng) + 大 (big). Growing big — growing up.
搬 (move — 扌hand + 般 carry) + 家 (home). To move the home — moving house.
土 (earth/soil) + 成 (completed). Earth built up into completed walls — city walls, by extension a walled city.
木 (tree) + 寸 (inch/small measure). A small cluster of trees and homes — a village.
祖 (ancestor — 礻altar + 且 stack of offerings, ancestors honored at the altar) + 国 (country). The country of one's ancestors — motherland.
